DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS ALEX SALAZAR, PAOLA SALAZAR'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE. PLAINTIFFS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO THEIR SET ONE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED AS TO REQUEST 30 AND DENIED AS TO REQUESTS 8, 16, 23-29 AND 31. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFFS' MOVING PAPERS SOUGHT FURTHER RESPONES FROM DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA FOR ALL 31 REQUESTS IN THEIR SET ONE PRODUCTION REQUESTS, IN THEIR REPLY MEMORANDUM THEY NARROWED THIS MOTION TO REQUESTS 8, 16, AND 23-31.(SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS)PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING AND DROP A HARD COURTESY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX. VISITING JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-20-2026 Continued To Aug-31-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2025 Continued To Feb-23-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-29-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Stephen A. Basinger, Esq. of Quill & Arrow, LLP, Tel: 310-933-4271 for plaintiff.
Jeff Fadeff, Esq., of GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP., Tel:(415) 986-5900 for defendant.

Hearing held. Defendant counsel contest the court's ruling granting the motion and ordering sanctions and requests that the Court to itemize the sanctions, as the case is approximately 99% settled. This will assist the parties in understanding what remains outstanding in order to reach a final settlement.The matter argued/discussed . The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs Alex Salazar and Paola Salazar's Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Defendants Person Most Knowledgeable And Production Of Documents; And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED.

The discovery at issue is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (CCP 2017.010.) If Defendant American Honda Motor Co., Inc.'s position is Plaintiffs have no legitimate interest in testimony from it on any of the 53 categories identified in the PMK deposition notice, then Defendant fundamentally misunderstands the case, the deposition notice and its duties under the California Discovery Act.

Plaintiffs have demonstrated adequate meet and confer.

Defendant has failed to demonstrate the merit of any of its numerous, mostly boilerplate objections to Plaintiffs' deposition notice. The only requests Defendant substantively discusses in its opposition are those related to "other vehicles." Plaintiffs' "other vehicle" requests regarding the same alleged defects is proper. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge]; see also .) Defendant's "obvious privacy concerns" are without support; in any event, Defendant has not demonstrated that it made any attempt to address, let alone resolve, such "obvious" concerns during meet and confer.

The deposition was set, ultimately, for June 11, 2025. "Covering counsel" being "called to trial" is not an excuse for non-attendance. If counsel had a conflict, it was up to Defendant to work it out or find coverage. Notice of unavailability the day before the stipulated date for a PMK deposition is unprofessional and rude.

Similarly, as to the related document demand, Plaintiff has demonstrated the requests are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence and Defendant has failed to demonstrate the merit of any objection. Defendant has not demonstrated that the court's February 28, 2025 Order bars the relief sought by this motion, or even that it demonstrates that this motion is an improper second bite at the apple. The court has reviewed the docket regarding that motion and is unpersuaded by Defendant's contention.

Defendant's PMK shall appear for deposition on all categories identified in the Notice and Defendant shall produce all requested documents by August 26, 2025. If there are privileged documents, a Code-complaint privilege log must be provided at the time of the production.

The record does not show Defendant acted with substantial justification with respect to this discovery. Defendant and counsel Spencer P. Hugret, Jeanette Suarez, Eric Sentlinger, Sarah Carlson Lambert, Reshma A. Bajaj and Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP, jointly and severally, shall pay $2,375 as sanctions, payable to Plaintiffs no later than August 6, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010, 2023.030 and 2025.450(g).)

Order is Signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Feb-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFs ALEX SALAZAR, PAOLA SALAZAR's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to their set one request for production of documents is granted as to request 30 and denied as to requests 8, 16, 23-29 and 31. Although plaintiffs' moving papers sought further respones from defendant American Honda for all 31 requests in their set one production requests, in their reply memorandum they narrowed this motion to requests 8, 16, and 23-31.

Plaintiffs' meet and confer effort was reasonable. Request 30 seeking "other vehicle" documents is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Donlen v. Ford Motor Co. (2013) 217 Cal.App.4th 138, 154 [explaining probative value of "other vehicle" evidence]; Santana v. FCA US, LLC (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 334, 346-347 [noting intentionally inadequate "repair" of fuel pump based on defendant's prior knowledge].) American Honda has not shown that it would be unduly burdensome for it to comply with request 30. American Honda must provide further responses and produce all responsive documents no later than March 28, 2025. Any documents withheld on the gorunds of privilege must be included in a privilege log.

American Honda's responses to requests 8, 16, 23-29 and 31 are both reasonable and code-compliant.

Plaintiff's counsel is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and drop a hard courtesy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
